sentence_reviews.text: API gateways and tool registries authenticate requests, describe callable capabilities, validate arguments, enforce policy, and route agents to external services.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: DDIA supports service/data-system routing and operations broadly; structured-output and NIST references support argument schemas and governance, but not this exact gateway/tool-registry definition.
sentence_reviews.notes: Definition is defensible from multiple sources, but a direct API gateway or agent tool-use source would be better.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of API gateways, tool registries & secure connectors is to connect Caches, APIs & tool interfaces, External databases, APIs & live tools to downstream capabilities including Conversational assistants & chatbots.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sources support external systems/tools broadly; downstream chatbot linkage is graph-derived.
sentence_reviews.notes: Plausible atlas positioning, not directly sourced.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Tool/action schema sources are relevant to assistants, but the exact substrate claim is not directly supported.
sentence_reviews.notes: Needs a direct agent tool-use or assistant connector citation.

sentence_reviews.text: combined with Caches, APIs & tool interfaces
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: DDIA supports caches and service interfaces generally, but not AI tool gateways specifically.

sentence_reviews.text: Operational gains introduce new failure modes in concurrency, versioning, security, numerical behavior, observability, and cost. Its direct dependencies include Caches, APIs & tool interfaces, External databases, APIs & live tools.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: DDIA and NIST support operational and security concerns generally; numerical behavior is not specific to gateways.
sentence_reviews.notes: Generic limitation template is only partially applicable.

sentence_reviews.text: API gateways, tool registries & secure connectors turns Caches, APIs & tool interfaces into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: DDIA supports caches and service interfaces as operational infrastructure.
sentence_reviews.notes: Core dependency is defensible; exact wording is templated.

sentence_reviews.text: API gateways, tool registries & secure connectors turns External databases, APIs & live tools into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.

sentence_reviews.notes: Core relationship is plausible for connector infrastructure.

sentence_reviews.text: API gateways, tool registries & secure connectors turns Privacy, governance, licensing & audit into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.

sentence_reviews.notes: Common dependency is defensible but not directly verified.

sentence_reviews.text: API gateways, tool registries & secure connectors turns Structured outputs & typed action generation into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Structured-output survey supports schemas and typed generation; gateway serving integration is a synthesis.
sentence_reviews.notes: Common dependency is plausible for typed tool calls.

sentence_reviews.text: API gateways, tool registries & secure connectors turns Cryptographic signing & tamper-evident logs into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: C2PA supports signatures/provenance; DDIA supports service operations, but gateway linkage is indirect.
sentence_reviews.notes: Common dependency is plausible for integrity-preserving connectors.

edge_reviews.notes: Gateway behavior naturally builds on API/tool interface infrastructure.
Routing to external APIs and live tools is central to the node.
Policy enforcement and audit make governance a plausible common dependency.
Typed action generation is relevant to tool registries and argument validation.
Signing and tamper-evident logging are plausible integrity mechanisms for secure connectors.
